titleStmt.title: Letter from Hugh de Fellenberg Montgomery to Edward Carson, 9 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Hugh de Fellenberg Montgomery

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Hugh de Fellenberg Montgomery (1844-1924). Montgomery was a landowner and politician. Originally a liberal and a strong supporter of Gladstone, Montgomery was also a firm unionist, but by 1916 he believed that Ulster unionists had no choice but to accept Lloyd George's proposal for a six-county Northern Ireland. The letter was addressed to Edward Carson (1854-1935). Carson was a lawyer and a politician. In 1916 he was at the peak of his parliamentary influence. It was in 1916 that he accepted the first lordship of the admiralty. In addition to this, Carson was also a leading unionist figure in Irish politics. He sanctioned the formation of the paramilitary Ulster Volunteer Force in January 1913; and he helped to found the Ulster provisional government of September 1913. This letter concerns nationalist and unionist politics. Montgomery writes about a nationalist repudiation of Redmond's provisional consent to the six counties plan. If, however, this does not occur, Montgomery authorizes Carson to enter into negotiations on a six county basis. Montgomery states that any resolution resulting from these negotiations are a result of the stresses of war and cannot be held as binding. Montgomery goes on to express his opinion that Home Rule is an act of treason and a breach of his oath of allegiance.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
copy
Private 9th June, 1916. My dear Carson, The contents of this day's Nationalists papers look
as if we might have a repudiation of Redmond's provisional consent
to the six Counties plan before Monday, which would save us from
a considerable difficulty. If this does not occur it will be obviously right
tactics for us to authorise you to enter upon negotiations on
the six counties basis. I hope, however, that you will arrange to have a
resolution proposed which will make it clear that we only do this
under great stress on account of the war, & that we are not
bound by what we do on Monday under any other circumstance that
may arise. The resolution should contain some plausible excuse
(1) for breaking or stretching the terms of the Covenant (2)
(which I think quite as important) for giving even a n co i mplied assent
to the setting up of Home Rule for any part of Ireland at present,
involving as it does most serious danger to the Realm and with which
even implicit consent appears to me to amount to an act of treason
which requires a very valid excuse. I feel that it is as big
a breach of my oath of allegiance as the abtaining abandoning of the three
counties is of the covenant! I attended the Unionists Alliance meeting yesterday
Some of them had been seeing Lloyd George and had received certain
offers from him, after considering which they passed the
resolution in to-day's papers. I got them to tone one part of
it which I think thought very unwise down a little, but did not try to
prevent them issuing it. Not knowing, however what sort of
resolution I might find myself obliged to vote for on Monday
I left the room before the resolution was put and they have consider-
ately omitted my name from the list of those present. Yours very truly, Hugh Montgomery. Monday's resolution shd also contain a strong instruction
to you to get the best safequards you can for unionists
outside the 6 counties — not to ginger you up -
but to show Southern Unionists that we are not
forgetting them.
